fb_text:
I run a commercial plant service company in Atlanta — 50+ corporate and commercial properties across the metro, real routes, a crew in the field.

Every day used to sound like this: "What's the gate code for the Riverside office?" "Where's the water shut-off?" "Did the hotel say anything about the lobby plants?"

My phone was the company filing cabinet, and everyone kept calling to open it.

So I built the fix for my own business. My crew texts notes between stops, the same way they already text me. FieldNotes turns those notes into service logs and a memory of every account — gate codes, access notes, client requests, all of it.

Now when my new guy needs the gate code for Riverside, he texts the bot instead of me. Gets the answer in seconds. My phone stays in my pocket.

He's been on for weeks. He never calls me anymore.

I didn't build this to sell software. I built it so I could get through a day without being the help desk. If your phone is the filing cabinet for your business, you know exactly what I'm talking about.

Ask it the gate code yourself — 60-second demo, no install, no credit card:
fieldnotesapp.io/app/try.html

Workers text notes between stops like they already do. FieldNotes turns them into service logs and instant answers about every account.
